---
title: "AMS597 Project Data Cleaning"
author: "Group 1"
date: "2026-03-12"
output: 
  pdf_document:
    number_sections: true
---

# Read Data
```{r}
setwd("~/glassdoor-company-reviews-ams")
df <- read.csv("data/processed/chatgpt_glassdoor_reviews_with_sector_revised.csv")
head(df)
```


# Missing
```{r}
setwd("~/glassdoor-company-reviews-ams")
source("src/utils/data_helpers.R")
Missing.Check.By.Column(df)
```


# Process Employee Status

If ```employee_length``` is missing, then delete it. (4 rows)

If is current employee, then use rating_year instead of end_year to minus employee_length to calculate the start_year. After this step we make sure all missing of end_year are currently employees.

```{r}

library(tidyverse)
library(lubridate)

df <- df %>% 
  drop_na(employee_length)

df$rating_year = as.numeric(sub("[^0-9]*(\\d{4}).*", "\\1", df$rating_date))

df[is.na(df$employee_type), "employee_type"] <- "Former"
# fill it as Former since they're all 
# todo  make it better: check whether endyear exists

df$employee_job_quit <- ifelse(grepl("^Former", df$employee_type, ignore.case = TRUE),TRUE, FALSE)

df$employee_job_start_year <- ifelse(
  df$employee_job_quit,
  df$employee_job_end_year - df$employee_length, # Former use the year quit
  df$rating_year - df$employee_length # Current: use the year of rating
)

lengthvars <- c("employee_job_start_year", "employee_job_end_year", "employee_job_quit", "employee_length", "employee_type")
print(df[, lengthvars])

# is there anyone quit job without giving end year? no
# all missing of employee_job_end_year due to not quit job
df[df$employee_job_quit & is.na(df$employee_job_end_year), ]

Missing.Check.By.Column(df)
```
